name: Diverse stochasticity adaptive foraging reference

source.paper: Shiraishi et al. 2018/2019, Diverse Stochasticity Leads a Colony of Ants to Optimal Foraging

notes: The paper reports that the best stochasticity distribution can shift with feeding environment.
Use this target to test whether heterogeneous trail-following noise helps adaptation after food relocation.

Raw trip totals and normalized adaptation ratios must be interpreted separately: low-noise colonies can have higher absolute exploitation, while mixed or medium-noise colonies can retain more of their own baseline efficiency after relocation.

relocated_early_trips_vs_initial.target_relation: diverse and medium > low and high
relocated_early_trips_vs_initial.description: After food relocation, heterogeneous or intermediate stochasticity should retain more of its initial foraging efficiency than low-noise trail lock-in or high-noise wandering.

raw_relocated_total_trips.description: Raw total trips may remain highest for low-noise colonies because stable trail exploitation is efficient once a new route is found.

current_v1_result.interpretation: Mismatch: low-noise ants outperform diverse stochasticity in the current model.

current_v2_result.interpretation: Partial alignment: diverse and medium profiles retain more of their own baseline efficiency immediately after relocation, consistent with adaptive flexibility. The model still predicts low-noise colonies have the highest absolute trip totals because route exploitation dominates after discovery.

changes: Added coarse traffic-density cost to movement, pheromone deposition and resource pickup throughput.
Added food-memory strength and decay so obsolete food memories fade with time, stochasticity and congestion.
current_v3_result.interpretation: Improved partial alignment: low-noise colonies still have the highest absolute total trips, but their advantage is reduced by crowding costs, and diverse/medium profiles retain more early post-relocation efficiency relative to their own baseline.

changes: Added state-dependent exploration drive while preserving each ant's base stochasticity profile.
Exploration drive rises under congestion, stale food memory and resource stress, and falls in exploitation contexts.
current_v4_result.interpretation: Partial alignment: diverse exceeds low on early relocation adaptation ratio, while medium is not clearly separated. Raw total exploitation remains highest for low-noise colonies.
